title: Mobile device with a short circuit switch to processes a wireless communication and a wireless charging application
abstract: Mobile device (16) to enable a wireless communication application with another device and to enable an energy harvesting application to charge a battery (17) or to power a processing stage of the mobile device (16), both applications based on a magnetic field with a specified carrier frequency in the HF frequency area, which mobile device (16) comprises an integrated circuit (18) with: a communication stage (19) that comprises a modulator (20) and a demodulator (21) built to enable load modulated wireless communication with the other device and with a harvesting stage (22) built for wireless harvesting of energy for the mobile device (16), which harvesting stage (22) comprises a rectifier (23) to provide a rectified DC-Voltage (VDDC), which rectifier (23) is connected via a first pin (27) and a second pin (28) of the integrated circuit (18) to a matching circuit (26) of the mobile device (16), which matching circuit (26) is connected with an antenna (25) of the mobile device (16), wherein that the communication stage (19) and the harvesting stage (22) are built to process the magnetic field with the same specified carrier frequency for both applications and, wherein the modulator (20) and the demodulator (21) are connected via a third pin (31) and a fourth pin (32) of the integrated circuit (18) directly to the antenna (25) and, that the integrated circuit (18) comprises a switch (33) connected parallel and directly to the rectifier (23) and directly with the first pin (27) and the second pin (28) of the integrated circuit (18) and, wherein the switch (33) is switched in its connecting status to short circuit the first pin (27) and the second pin (28) to enable the wireless communication application with only parallel to the antenna (25) arranged capacities (29, 30) in the matching circuit (26) and, wherein the switch (33) is switched in its disconnecting status to enable the charge application with only serial to the antenna (25) arranged capacities (29, 30) in the matching circuit (26).
claims: Claims 1. Mobile device (16) to enable a wireless communication application with another device and to enable an energy harvesting application to charge a battery (17) or to power a processing stage of the mobile device (16), both applications based on a magnetic field with a specified carrier frequency in the HF frequency area, which mobile device (16) comprises an integrated circuit (18) with: a communication stage (19) that comprises a modulator (20) and a demodulator (21) built to enable load modulated wireless communication with the other device and with a harvesting stage (22) built for wireless harvesting of energy for the mobile device (16), which harvesting stage (22) comprises a rectifier (23) to provide a rectified DC-Voltage (VDDC), which rectifier (23) is connected via a first pin (27) and a second pin (28) of the integrated circuit (18) to a matching circuit (26) of the mobile device (16), which matching circuit (26) is connected with an antenna (25) of the mobile device (16), characterized in that the communication stage (19) and the harvesting stage (22) are built to operate with the same specified carrier frequency for both applications and, wherein the modulator (20) and the demodulator (21) are connected via a third pin (31) and a fourth pin (32) of the integrated circuit (18) directly to the antenna (25) and, that the integrated circuit (18) comprises a switch (33) connected parallel and directly to the rectifier (23) and connected directly with the first pin (27) and the second pin (28) of the integrated circuit (18) and, wherein the switch (33) is switched in its connecting status to short circuit the first pin (27) and the second pin (28) to enable the wireless communication application with only parallel to the antenna (25) arranged capacities (29, 30) in the matching circuit (26) and, wherein the switch (33) is switched in its disconnecting status to enable the charge application with only serial to the antenna (25) arranged capacities (29, 30) in the matching circuit (26). 2. Mobile device (16) according to claim 1, wherein the matching circuit comprises only two capacities, namely the first capacity (29) and the second capacity (30). 3. Mobile device (16) according to any of the claims 1 or 2, wherein the communication stage is built to communicate with the other device although the switch (33) is switched in its disconnecting status to enable the charge application. 4. Mobile device (16) according to any of the claims 1 to 3, wherein harvesting stage (22) comprises a battery charger (24) powered by the rectified DC-Voltage (VDDC) and connected to the battery (17) to use the harvested energy to charge the battery (17). 5. Mobile device (16) according to any of the claims 1 to 4, wherein harvesting stage (22) is connected to a power pin (40) of the integrated circuit (18) to provide the rectified DC- Voltage (VDDC) at the power pin (40) and to use the harvested energy to power the processing stage of the mobile device (16). 6. Method to enable a wireless communication application with another device and to enable a charge application to charge a battery (17) of the mobile device (16) both applications based on a magnetic field with the same specified carrier frequency in the HF frequency area, which method is processed with a mobile device (16) according to one of the claims 1 to 4 and comprises the following steps: Activate the connecting status of a switch (33) to enable an increased communication range for the communication application; Communicate with another device in the same magnetic field and decide whether or not the charge application should be started to charge the battery (17) of the mobile device (16); Activate the disconnecting status of the switch (33) to enable high power charging of the battery (17) with the energy transferred in the magnetic field during the charge application, if it has been decided to charge the battery (17). 7. Method according to claim 6, which furthermore comprises the following step: Activate the connecting status of the switch (33) to enable an increased communication range for the communication application, after the battery (17) is fully charged. 8. Method according to any of the claims 6 or 7, which furthermore comprises the following step: Communicate with the other device in the same magnetic field with reduced communication range while the switch (33) is in its disconnected status during the charge application.
